contributor: university of north carolina at chapel hill library, chapel hill, nc
description: Friday August 23 1957 Felix Hickersons Trip to the West Coast Felix Hickerson got back home Sunday night from his trip to the West Coast At the request of the Weekly he wrote the fol lowing report In Richmond my first stop I met several engineers of the Virginia Department of High ways In Washington I visited the Bureau of Public Roads and the American Association of State Highway Officials The newly appointed Federal High way Administrator Berham D Tailamy was my classmate at Columbia University years ago in the graduate school of high way engineering I was glad to ee him again Three developments still in progress pose pressing problems and a challenge to the highway engineers of the nation 1 the science of aeria photography and programmetry 12 the marvelous electronic computers i i the 50billion dollar federal highway program for art inter state nonaccess highway sys tem Thi program calls for ex penditures that will rank next to those of World War 11 Mistakes in the layout of highways would indeed become colossal blunders Ls highways were improperly lo i ted with inadequate rights of way future changes would en tail vast y greater costs In reply
